channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources

item.title: 🚁 Trump says Iran downed US Apache helicopter near Strait of Hormuz, vows response
item.description: President Trump announced Iran shot down a US Army Apache helicopter over the Strait of Hormuz and declared the US "must, of necessity, respond." The two pilots were rescued unharmed by a Navy sea drone in what marks the first such rescue operation for the US military.

item.title: 🇪🇺 EU proposes 21st sanctions package targeting Russian banks and crypto networks
item.description: The European Union proposed its 21st round of sanctions against Russia, heavily targeting banks, crypto networks, drone production, and oil traders. EU chief diplomat Kaja Kallas announced the sweeping measures aimed at increasing pressure on Moscow over the Ukraine war.

item.title: ⬇️ Trump approval near record low as Americans brace for higher gas prices
item.description: A Reuters poll shows only 22% of Americans approve of Trump's handling of the cost of living, with most expecting gasoline prices to rise further amid the Iran conflict. The finding underscores political pressure on the administration as the Middle East crisis drags on.

item.title: 🇳🇬 Cholera outbreak in Nigeria's Borno state kills 74, infects over 7,000
item.description: A cholera outbreak in northeastern Nigeria's conflict-ridden Borno state has killed 74 people and infected more than 7,000 others since May. The outbreak adds to the humanitarian crisis in a region already devastated by years of insurgency.

item.title: 🐟 Russia faces cod export ban under proposed EU sanctions
item.description: Russian cod could be banned from EU markets in the latest sanctions package aimed at pressuring Moscow to end its invasion of Ukraine. The measure would target one of Russia's key export industries and further strain EU-Russia trade relations.

item.title: ⚠️ BBC reporter injured in Lebanon as Israel-Iran conflict intensifies
item.description: A BBC journalist was wounded in southern Lebanon amid escalating cross-border attacks between Israel and Iran-backed forces. The incident highlights the growing dangers for media covering the expanding regional conflict.

item.title: 🏛️ Why strongmen are wrong to loathe Europe
item.description: The Economist examines how Europe's democratic model continues to attract global admiration despite authoritarian leaders' attempts to discredit it. The analysis argues that the continent's soft power remains a potent counterweight to autocratic narratives.

item.title: 🌍 Middle East crisis live: Iran-Israel tensions escalate after Trump ceasefire call
item.description: Live coverage of the rapidly evolving Middle East situation as Iran and Israel exchange fire despite Trump's push for a ceasefire. The Strait of Hormuz remains a critical chokepoint with global oil supplies at risk.

item.title: 🔊 Israeli drones use sound of crying children to lure civilians in south Lebanon
item.description: Reports emerge that Israeli drones are broadcasting recordings of crying children to draw civilians out of hiding in southern Lebanon. The tactic has drawn condemnation from humanitarian organizations documenting the practice.

item.title: 💻 Microsoft cuts hundreds of cloud jobs in China as US tightens data laws
item.description: Microsoft is laying off hundreds of employees from its cloud division in mainland China amid tightening US data regulations and escalating tech tensions between Washington and Beijing.

item.title: 💰 EU to invest €5 billion in renewables across North Africa and Middle East
item.description: The European Union plans a major €5 billion investment in renewable energy projects across North Africa and the Middle East as part of efforts to reduce dependence on fossil fuels and strengthen energy partnerships.

item.title: 🇨🇳 Xi Jinping visits Korean War monument, evokes 'eternal historical memory'
item.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping's rare trip to North Korea included a visit to a Korean War memorial, emphasizing the "eternal historical memory" between the two nations. The visit comes amid shifting regional dynamics and US-China tensions.

item.title: 🇺🇸 Fed expected to hold rates as inflation concerns persist
item.description: The Federal Reserve is expected to maintain current interest rates as inflation remains stubborn amid the Iran conflict's impact on energy prices. Economists say rate cut hopes are fading as price pressures persist.

item.title: 🤖 China prepares $295 billion plan for nationwide AI infrastructure buildout
item.description: China is preparing a massive $295 billion investment plan to fund a nationwide artificial intelligence infrastructure buildout, aiming to compete with US dominance in the technology sector.

item.title: 🇰🇪 Kenyan police fire tear gas during protest against US Ebola quarantine facility
item.description: Kenyans protested against a US-funded Ebola quarantine facility in their community, with police using tear gas to disperse crowds. The incident highlights local opposition to international health infrastructure projects.

item.title: 🔐 Chinese hackers pose biggest espionage threat to tech firms, CrowdStrike warns
item.description: A new CrowdStrike report identifies Chinese state-sponsored hackers as the most significant espionage threat targeting technology companies globally. The warning comes amid escalating cyber tensions between major powers.

item.title: 🇵🇱 Poland should be involved in Ukraine talks, Tusk says
item.description: Polish Prime Minister Donald Tusk emphasized that Poland must be included in any negotiations concerning Ukraine's future, highlighting Warsaw's central role in regional security discussions.

item.title: 🇧🇬 Bulgaria's new government says it will not provide arms to Ukraine
item.description: Bulgaria's newly formed government announced it will not supply weapons to Ukraine, marking a shift in policy that could affect Western military support for Kyiv.

item.title: 🇬🇧 UK faces 'eye-watering' public sector cuts to fund defense pledges
item.description: Britain is preparing for severe public spending cuts to finance its defense commitments amid growing global security threats. The move reflects Europe's broader rearmament push in response to Russian aggression.

item.title: 🛢️ Oil prices fall 5% as Iran-Israel halt attacks following Trump ceasefire call
item.description: Global oil prices dropped sharply after Iran and Israel announced they had halted attacks on each other following US pressure for a ceasefire. Brent crude fell below $90 before recovering slightly above $92.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukraine conducts successful operations in Kursk region, claims territorial gains
item.description: Ukrainian forces report advances in Russia's Kursk region amid ongoing counteroffensive operations. The gains come as Kyiv continues to pressure Russian defensive positions along the border.

item.title: 🤝 Ukraine and Latvia sign drone cooperation deal, Zelenskyy announces
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy signed a drone technology cooperation agreement with Latvia during meetings with Nordic and Baltic leaders in Estonia, expanding military partnerships.

item.title: 🇨🇳 China and Georgia elevate ties to comprehensive strategic partnership
item.description: China and Georgia upgraded their bilateral relationship to a comprehensive strategic partnership, deepening Beijing's influence in the South Caucasus region amid growing geopolitical competition.

item.title: ⚔️ Russian attacks kill three in Ukraine's Kharkiv region including pregnant woman
item.description: Russian drone and missile strikes on Ukraine's northeastern Kharkiv region killed three civilians including a pregnant woman, local officials reported. The attacks continue a pattern of targeting civilian infrastructure.

item.title: 🇺🇳 UN chief Guterres calls for end to Middle East violence, urges Gaza crossings reopening
item.description: UN Secretary-General Antonio Guterres expressed deep alarm over renewed Middle East violence and called on Israel to reopen crossings into Gaza, warning of a worsening humanitarian crisis.
